date.1/1: is the day that Bulgaria officaly adopts the Euro, becoming the 21st Eurozone country.
date.1/2: is the day that Two aircraft collide on a runway at Haneda Airport in Tokyo, killing 5 and injuring 18.
date.1/3: is the day that The United States conducts airstrikes across northern Venezuela, including the capital Caracas and captures Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro and first lady Cilia Flores.
date.1/4: is the day that A fire in an escape room in Koszalin, Poland, kills five teenagers through carbon monoxide poisoning.
date.1/5: is the day that Alaska Airlines Flight 1282 makes an emergency landing at Portland International Airport after a door plug blows off the Boeing 737 MAX 9 operating the flight. There are no fatalities, but the accident prompts the 737 MAX to be grounded and renews scrutiny on Boeing's manufacturing and design issues.
date.1/6: is the day that Justin Trudeau announces his resignation as leader of the Liberal Party of Canada and Prime Minister of Canada after nine years in office.
date.1/7: is the day that A series of wildfires ravage the Greater Los Angeles area, resulting in at least 16 deaths and 13,401 structures destroyed.
date.1/8: is the day that Supporters of former Brazil president Jair Bolsonaro storm the Brazilian Congress.
date.1/9: is the day that Sriwijaya Air Flight 182 crashes north of Jakarta, Indonesia, killing all 62 people on board.
date.1/10: is the day that A 13-year-old American girl, Jayme Closs, is found alive in Gordon, Wisconsin, having been kidnapped 88 days earlier from her parents' home whilst they were murdered.
date.1/11: is the day that COVID-19 pandemic in Hubei: Municipal health officials in Wuhan announce the first recorded death from COVID-19.
date.1/12: is the day that Taal Volcano in the Philippines erupts and kills 39 people.
date.1/13: is the day that Outgoing U.S. President Donald Trump is impeached for a second time on a charge of incitement of insurrection following the January 6 United States Capitol attack one week prior.
date.1/14: is the day that Thirty-two people are killed when a crane falls onto a passenger train in Sikhio district, Thailand.
date.1/15: is the day that Yeti Airlines Flight 691 crashes near Pokhara International Airport, killing all 72 people on board.
date.1/16: is the day that The first impeachment of Donald Trump formally moves into its trial phase in the United States Senate.
date.1/17: is the day that Indonesia Air Transport ATR 42 crashed near Mount Bulusaraung in South Sulawesi, after losing contact en route to Makassar.
date.1/18: is the day that At least 45 people are killed and 292 others injured after two trains collide in Adamuz in the worst railway disaster in over a decade in Spain.
date.1/19: is the day that Bytedance and sister companies are banned from the United States for "security concerns".
date.1/20: is the day that Donald Trump is inaugurated as the 47th President of the United States of America. He is currently the oldest person ever inaugurated.
date.1/21: is the day that A fire at the Grand Kartal Hotel in the Kartalkaya ski resort in Bolu Province, Turkey, results in 78 people dead and 51 injured.
date.1/22: is the day that Ram Mandir is inaugurated by Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi at Ayodhya, Uttar Pradesh after 500 years of dispute.
date.1/23: is the day that Northwestern Air Flight 738 crashes after takeoff from Fort Smith Airport, Northwest Territories, Canada, killing six people.
date.1/24: is the day that At least 70 people are killed and 10 others missing after major landslides in West Java, Indonesia.
date.1/25: is the day that A mining company's dam collapses in Brumadinho, Brazil, a south-eastern city, killing 270 people.
date.1/26: is the day that Protesters and farmers storm the Red Fort near Delhi, clashing with police. One protester is killed and more than 80 police officers are injured.
date.1/27: is the day that Protests and public outrage spark across the U.S. after the release of multiple videos by the Memphis Police Department showing officers punching, kicking, and pepper spraying Tyre Nichols as a result of running away from a traffic stop, which resulted him dying in the hospital three days later after the incident.
date.1/28: is the day that A Learjet 45 crashes on approach to Baramati Airport in Maharashtra, killing all six occupants including the Deputy Chief Minister of Maharashtra, Ajit Pawar.
date.1/29: is the day that American Eagle Flight 5342 collided mid-air with a Sikorsky UH-60 Black Hawk operated by the United States Army and crashed into the Potomac River, killing all 67 people onboard both aircraft.
date.1/30: is the day that The World Health Organization declares the COVID-19 pandemic to be a Public Health Emergency of International Concern.
date.1/31: is the day that Med Jets Flight 056 crashes near Roosevelt Mall in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, killing 8 people and injuring 23.
date.2/1: is the day that Five-year-old Moroccan boy Rayan Aourram falls into a 32-meter (105 feet) deep well in Ighran village in Tamorot commune, Chefchaouen Province, Morocco, but dies four days later, before rescue workers reached him.
date.2/2: is the day that Slovenian NBA player Luka Doncic is traded from the Dallas Mavericks to the Los Angeles Lakers in exchange for Anthony Davis in one of the largest trades in american sports history.
date.2/3: is the day that Islamist militants massacred at least 162 while injuring and kidnapping dozens in two villages in Kwara State, Nigeria.
date.2/4: is the day that Ten people are killed in a mass shooting at an adult education centre in Örebro, Sweden.
date.2/5: is the day that United States President Donald Trump is acquitted by the United States Senate in his first impeachment trial.
date.2/6: is the day that Two earthquakes measuring Mww 7.8 and 7.5 struck near the border between Turkey and Syria with a maximum Mercalli intensity of XII (Extreme). The earthquakes resulted in numerous aftershocks and a death toll of 57,658 people.
date.2/7: is the day that Pakistan election offices are hit by twin bombings, killing at least 24 people a day before general elections.
date.2/8: is the day that Two children are killed and six others are injured when a bus crashes into a daycare centre in Laval, Quebec, Canada. The driver is arrested and charged with homicide and dangerous driving.
date.2/9: is the day that The Baltic states synchronize their electric power transmission infrastructure with the Continental Europe Synchronous Area (CESA), in objective to disconnect from the Russo-Belarussian agreement to use the IPS/UPS system.
date.2/10: is the day that Shootings at a residence and a school in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, Canada, leave nine people dead and 27 injured.
date.2/11: is the day that 2024 Finnish presidential election: Alexander Stubb is elected as the 13th president of Finland.
date.2/12: is the day that In the first general election since the July Revolution in Bangladesh, the Bangladesh Nationalist Party, led by Tarique Rahman, wins a landslide victory upon returning to power after almost 19 years. A referendum held alongside was also approved by the majority of voters.
date.2/13: is the day that Former U.S. President Donald Trump is acquitted in his second impeachment trial.
date.2/14: is the day that At least 22 people are killed in an attack on a village in Northwest Region, Cameroon.
date.2/15: is the day that Sixty people drown and hundreds are missing after a boat sinks on the Congo River near the village of Longola Ekoti, Mai-Ndombe Province, Democratic Republic of the Congo.
date.2/16: is the day that Five thousand people gathered in the town of Kherrata, Bejaia Province to mark the second anniversary of the Hirak protest movement. Demonstrations had been suspended because of the COVID-19 pandemic in Algeria.
date.2/17: is the day that Military vehicles explode outside a Turkish Armed Forces barracks in Ankara, Turkey, killing at least 29 people and injuring 61 others.
date.2/18: is the day that Perseverance, a Mars rover designed to explore Jezero crater on Mars, as part of NASA's Mars 2020 mission, lands successfully.
date.2/19: is the day that Mya Thwe Thwe Khine, a 19-year-old protester, becomes the first known casualty of anti-coup protests that formed in response to the 2021 Myanmar coup d'état.
date.2/20: is the day that Six people are killed and two injured in multiple shooting incidents in Kalamazoo County, Michigan.
date.2/21: is the day that In the prelude to the 2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine Russian President Vladimir Putin declares the Luhansk People's Republic and Donetsk People's Republic as independent from Ukraine, and moves troops into the region. The action is condemned by the United Nations.
date.2/22: is the day that Twosday, the name given to Tuesday, February 22, 2022, at 2:22:22, occurs.
date.2/23: is the day that A snap election is held in Germany.
date.2/24: is the day that Russo-Ukrainian War: Days after recognising Donetsk and Luhansk as independent states, Russian president Vladimir Putin orders the 2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine.
date.2/25: is the day that Four people are killed and several more are injured when Cuban Border Guard Troops confront and open fire on a US-registered speedboat violating Cuban waters.
date.2/26: is the day that A total of 279 female students aged between 10 and 17 are kidnapped by bandits in the Zamfara kidnapping in Zamfara State, Nigeria.
date.2/27: is the day that Pakistan Air Force JF-17 Thunder downs Indian pilot Abhinandan Varthaman's Mig-21 in an aerial dogfight and captures him after conducting airstrikes in Jammu and Kashmir.
date.2/28: is the day that The United States and Israel launch attacks across Iran, assassinating Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. Retaliatory strikes are launched by Iran against US military bases in the Gulf, with explosions reported in Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar and the UAE.
date.2/29: is the day that The Flour Massacre took place in the Gaza Strip: Israeli forces opened fire on Palestinians waiting for aid amidst the Gaza war, killing over 100 and wounding 750.
date.3/1: is the day that Thirty-five people are killed and 143 injured in a mass stabbing at Kunming Railway Station in China.
date.3/2: is the day that The Lebanese militant group Hezbollah launches several projectiles into northern Israel as a response to the assassination of Ali Khamenei, formally initiating the 2026 Lebanon War.
date.3/3: is the day that A bomb blast in Karachi, Pakistan, kills at least 48 people and injured 200 others in a predominantly Shia Muslim area.
date.3/5: is the day that The 2023 Estonian parliamentary election is held, with two centre-right liberal parties gaining an absolute majority for the first time.
date.3/6: is the day that 32 people are killed and 82 are injured when gunmen open fire on a ceremony in Kabul, Afghanistan. The Islamic State claims responsibility for the attack.
date.3/7: is the day that Sweden officially joins NATO, becoming its 32nd member.
date.3/8: is the day that International Women's Day marches in Mexico become violent with 62 police officers and 19 civilians injured in Mexico City alone.
date.3/9: is the day that A shooting in the Alsterdorf quarter of Hamburg, Germany, kills eight people and injures another eight.
date.3/10: is the day that Bam Adebayo of the Miami Heat recorded the  second-highest scoring game in NBA history with 83 Points.
date.3/11: is the day that The Burmese military kills at least 30 villagers, including 3 Buddhist monks, during the Pinlaung massacre in Shan State, Myanmar.
date.3/12: is the day that The United States suspends travel from Europe due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
date.3/13: is the day that President Donald Trump declares the COVID-19 pandemic to be a national emergency in the United States.
date.3/14: is the day that Burmese security forces kill at least 65 civilians in the Hlaingthaya massacre.
date.3/15: is the day that Four members of a Palestinian family are shot dead in Tammun by the Israeli military.
date.3/16: is the day that A series of Boko Haram bombings leave 26 dead and 146 injured in Maiduguri,  Nigeria.
date.3/17: is the day that Rojava conflict: At a conference in Rmelan, the Movement for a Democratic Society declares the establishment of the Democratic Federation of Northern Syria.
date.3/18: is the day that Israel launches widespread aerial bombardments and attacks on the Gaza Strip, killing at least 591 people, including children.
date.3/19: is the day that The Swiss Government brokers a deal for UBS to buy out rival Credit Suisse in an attempt to calm the 2023 banking crisis.
date.3/20: is the day that La Plume noire, an anarchist bookstore in Lyon, is targeted by an attack of the far-right, provoking a shock in Lyon's far-left circles.
date.3/21: is the day that China Eastern Airlines Flight 5735 crashes in Guangxi, China, killing 132 people.
date.3/22: is the day that At least 145 people are killed and 551 injured in a bombing and mass shooting at the Crocus City Hall in Krasnogorsk, Russia.
date.3/23: is the day that Israel Defense Forces kill 15 aid workers in the Rafah paramedic massacre.
date.3/24: is the day that The 2024 Senegalese presidential election is held following anti-government protests.
date.3/25: is the day that Syrian civil war: Following the completion of the Afrin offensive, the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) initiate an insurgency against the Turkish occupation of the Afrin District.
date.3/26: is the day that The Francis Scott Key Bridge collapses following a collision between the MV Dali container ship and one of the bridge's support pillars, killing 6 people.
date.3/27: is the day that Seven people, including the perpetrator, are killed in a mass shooting at the Covenant School in Nashville, Tennessee.
date.3/28: is the day that An earthquake strikes close to Mandalay, Myanmar with a magnitude of 7.7, killing over 100 people.
date.3/29: is the day that The ship Ever Given was dislodged from the Suez Canal.
date.3/30: is the day that Donald Trump becomes the first former United States president to be indicted by a grand jury.
date.3/31: is the day that A historic tornado outbreak occurs in the American Midwest and South.
date.4/1: is the day that The 2016 Nagorno-Karabakh conflict begins along the Nagorno-Karabakh Line of Contact.
date.4/2: is the day that Liberation Day tariffs: U.S. President Donald Trump announces sweeping worldwide tariffs.
date.4/3: is the day that YouTube headquarters shooting: A 38-year-old gunwoman opens fire at YouTube Headquarters in San Bruno, California, injuring three people before committing suicide.
date.4/4: is the day that The impeachment of President Yoon Suk Yeol of South Korea in response to his declaration of martial law is unanimously upheld by the country's Constitutional Court, ending his presidency.
date.4/5: is the day that Agents with the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement raid a slaughterhouse in Tennessee, detaining nearly 100 undocumented Hispanic workers in one of the largest workplace raids in the history of the United States.
date.4/6: is the day that A bus carrying the Humboldt Broncos junior ice hockey team collides with a semi-truck in Saskatchewan, Canada, killing 16 people and injuring 13 others.
date.4/7: is the day that Ketanji Brown Jackson is confirmed for the Supreme Court of the United States, becoming the first black female justice.
date.4/8: is the day that Solar eclipse of April 8, 2024: A total solar eclipse takes place at the Moon's ascending node, visible across North America.
date.4/9: is the day that Burmese military and security forces commit the Bago massacre, during which at least 82 civilians are killed.
date.4/10: is the day that A mass shooting occurs at the Old National Bank in Louisville, Kentucky that leaves five victims dead and eight wounded.
date.4/11: is the day that During the Pazigyi massacre, an airstrike conducted by the Myanmar Air Force kills at least 100 villagers in Pazigyi, Sagaing Region.
date.4/12: is the day that The Great Fire of Valparaíso ravages the Chilean city of Valparaíso, killing 16 people, displacing nearly 10,000, and destroying over 2,000 homes.
date.4/13: is the day that Rory McIlroy wins the Masters Tournament, becoming just the sixth person to complete the Grand Slam in golf.
date.4/14: is the day that Flooding in the Persian Gulf starts, killing 19 in Oman.
date.4/15: is the day that A mass shooting occurred at a Fedex Ground facility in Indianapolis, Indiana, killing nine and injuring seven.
date.4/16: is the day that The historic Børsen in Copenhagen, Denmark, is severely damaged by a fire.
date.4/17: is the day that The funeral of Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh, takes place at St George's Chapel, Windsor Castle.
date.4/18: is the day that A redacted version of the Mueller report is released to the United States Congress and the public.
date.4/19: is the day that The Ingenuity helicopter becomes the first aircraft to achieve flight on another planet.
date.4/20: is the day that SpaceX's Starship rocket, the largest and most powerful rocket ever built, launches for the first time. It explodes four minutes into flight.
date.4/21: is the day that Indonesian Navy submarine KRI Nanggala (402) sinks in the Bali Sea during a military drill, killing all 53 on board.
date.4/22: is the day that At least 26 people are killed in a terrorist attack on a group of tourists in Pahalgam, Jammu and Kashmir. The Resistance Front (TRF), an offshoot of the Pakistan-based militant group Lashkar-e-Taiba claimed responsibility for the attack.
date.4/23: is the day that The 2024 Lumut mid-air collision in Malaysia kills 10 people while rehearsing for the 90th anniversary of the Royal Malaysian Navy.
date.4/24: is the day that A mass stabbing at a school in Nantes, France, leaves 1 person dead and 3 others wounded.
date.4/25: is the day that At least 8,962 are killed in Nepal after a massive 7.8 magnitude earthquake strikes Nepal.
date.4/26: is the day that A car ramming attack at a Lapu-Lapu Day festival kills 11 people and injures at least 30 in Vancouver, Canada.
date.4/27: is the day that The Panmunjom Declaration is signed between North and South Korea, officially declaring their intentions to end the Korean conflict.
date.4/28: is the day that CBS News releases evidence of the Abu Ghraib torture and prisoner abuse. The photographs show rape and abuse from the American troops over Iraqi detainees.
date.4/29: is the day that A baseball game between the Baltimore Orioles and the Chicago White Sox sets the all-time low attendance mark for Major League Baseball. Zero fans were in attendance for the game, as the stadium was officially closed to the public due to the 2015 Baltimore protests.
date.4/30: is the day that Forty-five men and boys are killed in the Meron stampede in Israel.
date.5/1: is the day that The 2024 Loblaw boycott, a Canadian boycott against retail corporation and grocer Loblaw Companies, begins.
date.5/2: is the day that Two mudslides in Badakhshan, Afghanistan, leave up to 2,500 people missing.
date.5/3: is the day that Nine students and a security guard are killed in the Belgrade school shooting, the first attack of its kind in Serbia.
date.5/4: is the day that Nine people are killed and thirteen injured in a spree shooting in Mladenovac and Smederevo, Serbia. It is the second mass shooting in the country in two days.
date.5/5: is the day that The World Health Organization declares the end of the COVID-19 pandemic as a global health emergency.
date.5/6: is the day that The coronation of Charles III and Camilla as King and Queen of the United Kingdom and the other Commonwealth realms is held in Westminster Abbey, London.
date.5/7: is the day that The Indian Army and the Indian Air Force conduct surgical strikes code-named Operation SINDOOR on terrorist hideouts in Pakistan in response to the Pahalgam Attack that killed 26 people.
date.5/8: is the day that The 2025 papal conclave elects Cardinal Robert Francis Prevost, taking the name Leo XIV as the 267th Pope of the Catholic Church.
date.5/9: is the day that The May 9 riots following the arrest of Imran Khan in Pakistan.
date.5/10: is the day that Start of the May 2024 Solar Storms, the most powerful set of Geomagnetic storms since the 2003 Halloween solar storms.
date.5/11: is the day that Start/Middle of the May 2024 Solar Storms, the most powerful set of Geomagnetic storms since the 2003 Halloween solar storms.
date.5/12: is the day that Middle/End of the May 2024 Solar Storms, the most powerful set of Geomagnetic storms since the 2003 Halloween solar storms.
date.5/13: is the day that An explosion at an underground coal mine in southwest Turkey kills 301 miners.
date.5/14: is the day that Ten people are killed in a mass shooting in Buffalo, New York.
date.5/15: is the day that Prime Minister of Slovakia Robert Fico is shot and critically injured while meeting with supporters at an event in Handlová.
date.5/16: is the day that A devastating EF4 tornado kills nineteen people in Southeast Kentucky, hitting the towns of Somerset and London.
date.5/17: is the day that A military plane crash in northern Laos kills 17 people.
date.5/18: is the day that United States presidential election: Joe Biden launches his presidential campaign.
date.5/19: is the day that A helicopter crash in Iran leaves 8 people dead, including the country's president Ebrahim Raisi & foreign minister Hossein Amir-Abdollahian.
date.5/20: is the day that Russo-Ukrainian War: Russia claims full control of the Ukrainian city of Mariupol after a nearly three-month siege.
date.5/21: is the day that The Greenfield tornado kills 5 and injures 35 across rural Iowa, United States. Wind speeds in excess of 480 kilometres per hour (300 mph) are estimated from measurements for the third time in history.
date.5/22: is the day that Hypothermia kills 21 runners in the 100 km (60-mile) Gansu ultramarathon disaster in China.
date.5/23: is the day that Anthony Albanese of the Australian Labor Party is sworn in as the 31st Prime Minister of Australia after winning the 2022 Australian federal election, ending 9 years of conservative rule.
date.5/24: is the day that A mass shooting occurs at Robb Elementary School in Uvalde, Texas, United States, resulting in the deaths of 21 people, including 19 children.
date.5/25: is the day that The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) becomes enforceable in the European Union.
date.5/26: is the day that 65 people are injured when a car rams into a crowd on Water Street, near Liverpool F.C.'s Premier League trophy parade.
date.5/27: is the day that Maryland Flood Event: A flood occurs throughout the Patapsco Valley, causing one death, destroying the entire first floors of buildings on Main Street in Ellicott City, and causing cars to overturn.
date.5/28: is the day that Former Formula One driver Takuma Sato wins his first Indianapolis 500, the first Japanese and Asian driver to do so. Double world champion Fernando Alonso retires from an engine issue in his first entry of the event.
date.5/29: is the day that Tara Air Flight 197 crashes in Nepal's Mustang District, killing 22.
date.5/30: is the day that Donald Trump is convicted of falsifying business records in his New York trial, the first time a former President of the United States has been found guilty in a criminal case.
date.5/31: is the day that A shooting occurs inside a municipal building at Virginia Beach, Virginia, leaving 13 people dead, including the shooter, and four others injured.
date.6/1: is the day that A ship carrying 458 people capsizes in the Yangtze river in China's Hubei province, killing 442 people.
date.6/2: is the day that A collision between two passenger trains and a parked freight train near the city of Balasor, Odisha in eastern India, results in 296 deaths and more than 1,200 people injured.
date.6/3: is the day that Reconstitution of the Academy of the Distrustful in the Sala Dalmases of the Historical Archive of the City of Barcelona in Barcelona.
date.6/4: is the day that Eleven people are killed and 56 people are injured during a crowd crush incident outside M.Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru, India for the celebration of Royal Challengers Bengaluru's Indian Premier League victory.
date.6/5: is the day that The Nintendo Switch 2 video game console is released worldwide.
date.6/6: is the day that The launch of SpaceX Starship integrated flight test 4 (IFT-4)
date.6/7: is the day that A Myanmar Air Force Shaanxi Y-8 crashes into the Andaman Sea near Dawei, Myanmar, killing all 122 aboard.
date.6/8: is the day that Former US President Donald Trump is indicted on federal charges of misusing classified information.
date.6/9: is the day that At least 40 people are killed and more than 70 wounded in a suicide bombing at a wedding party in Arghandab, Kandahar.
date.6/10: is the day that Eleven people are killed, including the perpetrator, and eleven others are injured, in a mass shooting at a secondary school in Graz, Austria.
date.6/11: is the day that Greece's public broadcaster ERT is shut down by then-prime minister Antonis Samaras. It would be opened exactly two years later by then-prime minister Alexis Tsipras.
date.6/12: is the day that Air India Flight 171, a Boeing 787-8 Dreamliner, crashes shortly after takeoff into the B. J. Medical College, Ahmedabad, India, killing 241 out of 242 onboard as well as 19 on the ground. This marked the first fatal crash and hull loss of the Boeing 787 Dreamliner.
date.6/13: is the day that Israeli strikes on Iran: Israel initiates air strikes against Iran.
date.6/14: is the day that The Grenfell Tower fire, a catastrophic fire in a high-rise apartment building in North Kensington, London, UK, leaves 72 people dead and another 74 injured.
date.6/15: is the day that A bomb explodes on a bus in the Pakistani city of Quetta, killing at least 25 people and wounding 22 others.
date.6/16: is the day that Upwards of 2,000,000 people participate in the 2019–20 Hong Kong protests, the largest in Hong Kong's history.
date.6/17: is the day that Juneteenth National Independence Day, was signed into law by President Joe Biden, to become the first federal holiday established since Martin Luther King Jr. Day in 1983.
date.6/18: is the day that Titan, a submersible operated by OceanGate Expeditions, imploded while attempting to view the wreck of the Titanic, killing all five people on board including OceanGate co-founder and CEO Stockton Rush in the North Atlantic Ocean.
date.6/19: is the day that Animal rights advocate Regan Russell is run over and killed by a transport truck outside of a pig slaughterhouse in Burlington, Ontario.
date.6/20: is the day that The first EF5 tornado in 12 years occurs in Enderlin, North Dakota.
date.6/21: is the day that A hot air balloon catches fire mid-flight and crashes in Praia Grande, Santa Catarina, Brazil, killing 8 of the 21 on board.
date.6/22: is the day that The United States conducts airstrikes on three Iranian nuclear sites in Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan.
date.6/23: is the day that Twelve boys and an assistant coach from a soccer team in Thailand are trapped in a flooding cave, leading to an 18-day rescue operation.
date.6/24: is the day that The Wagner Group led by Yevgeny Prigozhin launches an insurrection against the Russian government.
date.6/25: is the day that Thousands of people storm Kenya's Parliament Buildings protesting the passing of the government's 2024/25 Finance Bill.
date.6/26: is the day that Julian Assange, founder of WikiLeaks, returns to Australia after pleading guilty to one charge of espionage in a Saipan court and subsequently being released by the United States Department of Justice.
date.6/27: is the day that U.S. President Joe Biden debates former U.S. President Donald Trump. Biden's perceived poor performance leads to his withdrawal from the election on July 21.
date.6/28: is the day that A terrorist attack in Turkey's Istanbul Atatürk Airport kills 42 people and injures more than 230 others.
date.6/29: is the day that The Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant self-declares its caliphate in Syria and northern Iraq.
date.6/30: is the day that A Tajik citizen with ISIS connections, wanted in Tajikistan for murder and kidnapping, kills two people at Chișinău International Airport in Moldova, after being denied entry to the country.
date.7/1: is the day that At the centennial ceremony of the Dominion of Newfoundland National War Memorial, the Commonwealth War Graves Commission allowed an unprecedented second Canadian Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. The Royal Newfoundland Regiment soldier was entombed in the memorial at this ceremony.
date.7/2: is the day that A stampede during a religious event in Uttar Pradesh, India, leaves at least 121 people dead and 150 others injured.
date.7/3: is the day that President of Egypt Mohamed Morsi is removed from office by the military after four days of protests all over the country calling for his resignation, to which he did not respond. The president of the Supreme Constitutional Court of Egypt, Adly Mansour, is declared acting president until further elections are held.
date.7/4: is the day that A devastating flood strikes the Texas Hill Country, killing at least 108 people.
date.7/5: is the day that Keir Starmer is appointed Prime Minister by Charles III, becoming the first Labour prime minister since Gordon Brown in 2010 and the first one to win a general election since Tony Blair at the 2005 general election
date.7/6: is the day that The Georgia Guidestones, a monument in the United States, are heavily damaged in a bombing, and are dismantled later the same day.
date.7/7: is the day that Boris Johnson announces his resignation as leader of the Conservative Party following days of pressure from the Members of Parliament (MPs) during the July 2022 United Kingdom government crisis.
date.7/8: is the day that Former Japanese prime minister Shinzo Abe is shot and killed with an improvised firearm due to resentment against the Unification Church.
date.7/9: is the day that Earth completes its shortest recorded day due to a slight acceleration in rotation, with July 9 lasting approximately 1.3 to 1.6 milliseconds less than 24 hours.
date.7/10: is the day that The final Volkswagen Beetle rolls off the line in Puebla, Mexico; the last of 5,961 "Special Edition" cars will be exhibited in a museum.
date.7/11: is the day that Virgin Galactic launches its founder, Richard Branson, into space, the first company ever to do so.
date.7/12: is the day that Gazpromavia Flight 9608 crashes in Russia's Kolomensky District near Kolomna, killing three.
date.7/13: is the day that Former president of the United States Donald Trump is injured in an assassination attempt while speaking at an election campaign rally near Butler, Pennsylvania.
date.7/14: is the day that A GippsAero GA8 Airvan crashes in Umeå, Sweden, killing all nine aboard.
date.7/15: is the day that France win their second World Cup title, defeating Croatia 4–2.
date.7/16: is the day that A 100-year-old building in Mumbai, India, collapses, killing at least 10 people and leaving many others trapped.
date.7/17: is the day that At least 120 people are killed and 130 injured by a suicide bombing in Diyala Governorate, Iraq.
date.7/18: is the day that A man sets fire to an anime studio in Fushimi-ku, Kyoto, Japan, killing 36 people and injuring dozens of others.
date.7/19: is the day that The Knesset passes the controversial Nationality Bill, which defines the State of Israel as the nation-state of the Jewish people.
date.7/20: is the day that American businessman Jeff Bezos flies to space aboard New Shepard NS-16 operated by his private spaceflight company Blue Origin.
date.7/21: is the day that A Bangladesh Air Force Chengdu FT-7BGI crashes shortly after takeoff into Milestone School campus in Dhaka, Bangladesh, killing 35 people and injuring 173.
date.7/22: is the day that Chandrayaan-2, the second lunar exploration mission developed by Indian Space Research Organisation after Chandrayaan-1 is launched from Satish Dhawan Space Centre in a GSLV Mark III M1. It consists of a lunar orbiter, and also included the Vikram lander, and the Pragyan lunar rover.
date.7/23: is the day that A wildfire in East Attica kills at least 102 people. It is the deadliest wildfire in the history of Greece.
date.7/24: is the day that Angara Airlines Flight 2311 crashes on approach to Tynda Airport, killing all 48 people on board.
date.7/25: is the day that National extreme heat records set this day in the UK, Belgium, The Netherlands and Germany during the July 2019 European heat wave.
date.7/26: is the day that The Sagamihara stabbings occur in Kanagawa Prefecture in Japan. Nineteen people are killed.
date.7/27: is the day that At least seven people are killed and many injured after gunmen attack an Indian police station in Punjab.
date.7/28: is the day that Catastrophic floods devastate Eastern Kentucky, resulting in 45 fatalities and causing damage to thousands of homes and businesses.
date.7/29: is the day that Three children are stabbed to death and 10 other people injured at a dance studio in Southport, England. This incident, coupled with widespread online misinformation, leads to various racially motivated riots across the UK.
date.7/30: is the day that A magnitude 8.8 earthquake hits Russia, causing tsunamis over the Pacific Ocean.
date.7/31: is the day that Gas explosions in the southern Taiwanese city of Kaohsiung kill at least 20 people and injure more than 270.
date.8/1: is the day that Former US President Donald Trump is indicted for his role in the January 6 United States Capitol attack, his third indictment in 2023.
date.8/2: is the day that At least 146 people were killed and more than 114 injured in a factory explosion in Kunshan, Jiangsu, China.
date.8/3: is the day that Worst floods hit major parts of Slovenia.
date.8/4: is the day that Beirut Port explosion: At least 220 people are killed and over 5,000 are wounded when 2,700 tons of ammonium nitrate explodes in Beirut, Lebanon.
date.8/5: is the day that Following the non-cooperation movement against the government of Bangladesh, Bangladeshi prime minister Sheikh Hasina resigns and flees the country, ending her rule of 15 consecutive years and a total of almost two decades. The date is also known as 36 July.
date.8/6: is the day that A suicide bomb attack kills at least 15 people at a mosque in the Saudi city of Abha.
date.8/7: is the day that Air India Express Flight 1344 overshoots the runway at Calicut International Airport in the Malappuram district of Kerala, India, and crashes, killing 21 of the 190 people on board.
date.8/8: is the day that Nobel laureate Muhammad Yunus takes oath as Chief Adviser to form an interim government in Bangladesh.
date.8/9: is the day that Voepass Linhas Aéreas Flight 2283 crashes near Vinhedo, São Paulo, killing all 62 people on board.
date.8/10: is the day that Israel strikes Al-Tabaeen school in eastern Gaza City, killing at least 80 Palestinians.
date.8/11: is the day that Luna 25 launches from the Vostochny Cosmodrome.
date.8/12: is the day that Six people, five victims and the perpetrator are killed in Keyham, Plymouth in the worst mass shooting in the UK since 2010.
date.8/13: is the day that Israel–United Arab Emirates relations are formally established.
date.8/14: is the day that Former U.S. president Donald Trump is charged in Georgia along with 18 others in attempting to overturn the results of the 2020 election in that state, his fourth indictment of 2023.
date.8/15: is the day that US president Donald Trump meets with Russian president Vladimir Putin in Alaska, the first such summit since the 2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine.
date.8/16: is the day that The August Complex fire in California burns more than one million acres of land.
date.8/17: is the day that A bomb explodes at a wedding in Kabul killing 63 people and leaving 182 injured.
date.8/18: is the day that One hundred activists, officials, and other concerned citizens in Iceland hold a funeral for Okjökull glacier, which has completely melted after having once covered six square miles (15.5 km2).
date.8/19: is the day that Tens of thousands of farmed non-native Atlantic salmon are accidentally released into the wild in Washington waters in the 2017 Cypress Island Atlantic salmon pen break.
date.8/20: is the day that Joe Biden gives his acceptance speech virtually for the 2020 Democratic presidential nomination at the 2020 Democratic National Convention.
date.8/21: is the day that A solar eclipse traverses the continental United States.
date.8/23: is the day that Chandrayaan-3 mission initiated first Moon landing in Indian history.
date.8/24: is the day that Japan officially begins discharging treated radioactive water from the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant into the Pacific Ocean, sparking international concerns and condemnation.
date.8/25: is the day that Hurricane Harvey makes landfall in Texas as a powerful Category 4 hurricane, the strongest hurricane to make landfall in the United States since 2004.
date.8/26: is the day that Exactly 5 years after the 2018 Jacksonville Landing shooting, there is another shooting in Jacksonville, Florida, leaving 3 people dead.
date.8/27: is the day that Hurricane Irene strikes the United States east coast, killing 47 and causing an estimated $15.6 billion in damage.
date.8/28: is the day that Phoenix shooting: A man opens fire on pedestrians outside of a hotel in Phoenix, Arizona, resulting in the deaths of three people, including the perpetrator.
date.8/29: is the day that Russo-Ukrainian War: Ukraine begins its southern counteroffensive in the Kherson Oblast, eventually culminating in the liberation of the city of Kherson.
date.8/30: is the day that Gabonese coup d'état: After Ali Bongo Ondimba's reelection, a military coup ousted him, ending 56 years of Bongo family rule in Gabon.
date.9/1: is the day that Iraq War: The United States Armed Forces transfers control of Anbar Province to the Iraqi Armed Forces.
date.9/2: is the day that Four people are killed in a mass shooting targeting homeless people on a Chicago Transit Authority train in Forest Park, Illinois, United States.
date.9/3: is the day that North Korea conducts its sixth and most powerful nuclear test.
date.9/4: is the day that A 14-year-old gunman kills four people and injures seven in a mass shooting at Apalachee High School near Winder, Georgia.
date.9/5: is the day that Liz Truss is declared the winner of the UK Conservative Party leadership election, beating Rishi Sunak.
date.9/6: is the day that Boris Johnson resigns as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom, and is replaced by Liz Truss. Their meetings with Queen Elizabeth II at Balmoral Castle were the Queen's final official duties before her death two days later.
date.9/7: is the day that Bitcoin becomes legal tender in El Salvador.
date.9/10: is the day that American right-wing political activist Charlie Kirk is assassinated while onstage at Utah Valley University in Orem, Utah.
date.9/11: is the day that Hurricane Francine impacts the Gulf of Mexico, as a Category 2 hurricane.
date.9/12: is the day that Siberian Light Aviation Flight 51 crashes short of the runway at Kazachinskoye Airport, killing four.
date.9/13: is the day that Taliban insurgents attack the United States consulate in Herat, Afghanistan, with two members of the Afghan National Police reported dead and about 20 civilians injured.
date.9/14: is the day that Death of Queen Elizabeth II: The Queen's coffin is taken from Buckingham Palace, placed on a gun carriage of The King's Troop Royal Horse Artillery and moved in a procession to Westminster Hall for her lying in state over the next four days with the queue of mourners stretching for miles along the River Thames.
date.9/15: is the day that Signing of the Bahrain–Israel normalization agreement occurs in Washington, D.C., normalizing relations between Israel and two Arab nations, the United Arab Emirates and Bahrain.
date.9/16: is the day that During the Let Yet Kone massacre, the Burmese military kills 13 villagers, including eight children, after attacking a school in Sagaing Region, Myanmar.
date.9/17: is the day that Saudi Arabia and Pakistan sign the Strategic Mutual Defence Agreement.
date.9/18: is the day that Cannabis is legalized in South Africa, through a ruling of the Constitutional Court.
date.9/19: is the day that Azerbaijan launches a military offensive against the Republic of Artsakh in the Nagorno-Karabakh region; this leads to the flight of the Armenian population.
date.9/20: is the day that Roughly four million people, mostly students, demonstrate across the world to address climate change. Sixteen-year-old Greta Thunberg from Sweden leads the demonstration in New York City.
date.9/21: is the day that A 5.6 Mw earthquake shakes the Albanian port of Durrës. Forty-nine people are injured in the capital, Tirana.
date.9/22: is the day that Anura Kumara Dissanayake is elected as the 9th President Of Sri Lanka.
date.9/23: is the day that Israel launches airstrikes against Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, killing more than 490 people.
date.9/24: is the day that NASA's OSIRIS-REx capsule containing samples from the asteroid 101955 Bennu successfully lands back on Earth.
date.9/25: is the day that Bill Cosby is sentenced to three to ten years in prison for aggravated sexual assault.
date.9/26: is the day that Hurricane Helene makes landfall in Perry, Florida as a Category 4 hurricane, killing over 250 people, causing US$78.7 billion in damage and becoming the deadliest hurricane in the mainland United States since Katrina.
date.9/27: is the day that Second Nagorno-Karabakh War: Azerbaijan launches an offensive against the self-proclaimed Republic of Artsakh in the Nagorno-Karabakh region, inhabited predominantly by ethnic Armenians.
date.9/28: is the day that The 2023 Rotterdam shootings occurred, during which two people were killed in a shooting and arson incident at a residence in Delfshaven, Rotterdam. Additionally, one person lost their life in a classroom at the Erasmus University Medical Center.
date.9/29: is the day that Violence and low turnout mar the 2019 Afghan presidential election.
date.9/30: is the day that Hurricane Matthew becomes a Category 5 hurricane, making it the strongest hurricane to form in the Caribbean Sea since 2007.
date.10/1: is the day that Israel invaded Southern Lebanon, marking the fifth Israeli invasion of Lebanon since 1978.
date.10/2: is the day that 2 people are killed and at least 4 others injured in an attack on a synagogue in Manchester, UK, during Yom Kippur.
date.10/3: is the day that Bengali, Assamese, Marathi, Pali and Prakrit are accorded the Classical language status by the Government of India
date.10/4: is the day that Joint Nigerien-American Special Forces are ambushed by Islamic State militants outside the village of Tongo Tongo.
date.10/5: is the day that Windows 11 is released to the general public.
date.10/6: is the day that The 2025 Alberta teachers' strike begins, leaving approximately 51,000 teachers off-work, impacting about 730,000 Albertan students.
date.10/7: is the day that Hamas and several other Palestinian militant groups launch an attack into Israel, which results in the deaths of around 1,200, mostly civilians, and the taking of 251 hostages, including civilians and soldiers. The attack initiated the Gaza war and the larger Middle Eastern crisis.
date.10/8: is the day that Second Nagorno-Karabakh War: Azerbaijan twice deliberately targeted the Church of the Holy Savior Ghazanchetsots of Shusha.
date.10/9: is the day that Hurricane Milton makes landfall in Siesta Key, Florida, as a Category 3 hurricane, causing US$34.3 billion in damage only two weeks after Hurricane Helene impacted the state.
date.10/10: is the day that Ben S. Bernanke, Douglas W. Diamond and Philip H. Dybvig are jointly awarded the Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences.
date.10/11: is the day that Soyuz MS-10, launching an intended crew for the ISS, suffers an in-flight abort. The crew lands safely.
date.10/12: is the day that 2022 Bratislava shooting, killing 3 (including the perpetrator) and injuring one. The shooting occurred outside of a gay bar in Bratislava known as Tepláreň. Two people (excluding the perpetrator) died as a result of the shooting: Juraj Vankulič, a non-binary person, and Matúš Horváth, a bisexual man. The perpetrator (Juraj Krajčík) was found dead of a self-inflicted gunshot the morning after the attack.
date.10/13: is the day that Kenyan Brigid Kosgei sets a new world record for a woman runner with a time of 2:14:04 at the 2019 Chicago Marathon.
date.10/14: is the day that A coup d'état successfully overthrows Malagasy president Andry Rajoelina.
date.10/15: is the day that 13-year-old American girl, Jayme Closs, is kidnapped from her Barron, Wisconsin home after her parents were both murdered.
date.10/16: is the day that Storm Ophelia strikes the U.K. and Ireland causing major damage and power loss.
date.10/17: is the day that An explosion at Al-Ahli Arab Hospital in Gaza kills hundreds of Palestinians during the Gaza war.
date.10/18: is the day that NASA Astronauts Jessica Meir and Christina Koch take part in the first all-female spacewalk when they venture out of the International Space Station to replace a power controller.
date.10/19: is the day that Pieces of the French Crown Jewels are successfully stolen during a heist on the Louvre Museum in Paris..
date.10/20: is the day that Liz Truss steps down as British Prime Minister and leader of the Conservative Party amid the country's political crisis, serving for the least time of any British Prime Minister (49 days).
date.10/21: is the day that A shooting occurs on the set of the film Rust, in which actor Alec Baldwin discharged a prop weapon which had been loaded, killing the director of photography, Halyna Hutchins, and injuring director Joel Souza.
date.10/23: is the day that Xi Jinping is elected as General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party by the Central Committee, beginning a third term of the paramount leader of China.
date.10/24: is the day that The world's longest sea crossing, the Hong Kong–Zhuhai–Macau Bridge, opens for public traffic.
date.10/25: is the day that A mass shooting occurs in two locations in Lewiston, Maine. 18 people are killed and 13 more injured.
date.10/26: is the day that A 7.5 magnitude earthquake strikes in the Hindu Kush mountain range in South Asia, killing 399 people and leaving 2,536 people injured.
date.10/27: is the day that Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant founder and leader Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi kills himself and three children by detonating a suicide vest during the U.S. military Barisha raid in northwestern Syria.
date.10/28: is the day that Hurricane Melissa makes landfall near Black River, Jamaica, killing over 30 people, as well as tying the 1935 Labor Day hurricane as the most intense landfall in the North Atlantic.
date.10/29: is the day that At least 156 die at a crowd crush during a Halloween celebration in Itaewon district, Seoul, South Korea.
date.10/30: is the day that First rescue of a prisoner during the Israeli invasion of Gaza.
date.10/31: is the day that Berlin Brandenburg Airport opens its doors after nearly 10 years of delays due to construction issues and project corruption.
date.11/1: is the day that A concrete canopy collapses at the Novi Sad railway station, killing 16 people and injuring 3.
date.11/2: is the day that A peace agreement is signed between the Ethiopian government and the Tigray People's Liberation Front, ending the Tigray War.
date.11/3: is the day that The 2020 United States presidential election takes place between Democratic Joe Biden and Republican incumbent President Donald Trump. On November 7, Biden was declared the winner.
date.11/6: is the day that Syrian civil war: The Syrian Democratic Forces launch an offensive to capture the ISIL-held city of Raqqa.
date.11/7: is the day that António Costa resigns as Prime Minister of Portugal following news of an investigation in a corruption scandal implicating members of his cabinet.
date.11/8: is the day that Myanmar holds the 2020 general election, re-electing a government led by the National League for Democracy, which is deposed by the Burmese military the following February during the 2021 Myanmar coup d'état.
date.11/9: is the day that U.S. surgeons at NYU Langone Health announce the world's first whole eye transplant.
date.11/10: is the day that Armenia and Azerbaijan sign a ceasefire agreement, ending the Second Nagorno-Karabakh War, and prompting protests in Armenia.
date.11/11: is the day that A vehicle-ramming attack in Zhuhai, China, kills 38 people and injures 48.
date.11/12: is the day that A Boeing B-17 Flying Fortress and a Bell P-63 Kingcobra collide in mid-air over Dallas Executive Airport during an airshow, killing six.
date.11/13: is the day that A mass stabbing occurs in Moscow, Idaho in which four University of Idaho students are killed in off-campus housing.
date.11/14: is the day that A mass shooting occurs at Saugus High School in Santa Clarita, California, resulting in three deaths, including that of the perpetrator, and three injuries.
date.11/15: is the day that The world population reached eight billion.
date.11/16: is the day that Artemis Program: NASA launches Artemis 1 on the first flight of the Space Launch System, the start of the program's future missions to the moon.
date.11/17: is the day that The first known case of COVID-19 is traced to a 55-year-old man who had visited a market in Wuhan, Hubei Province, China.
date.11/18: is the day that The Utah monolith, built sometime in 2016 is discovered by state biologists of the Utah Division of Wildlife Resources.
date.11/19: is the day that The 2023 Cricket World Cup final takes place at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad, India, played between host nation India and Australia.
date.11/20: is the day that The 2022 FIFA World Cup begins in Qatar. This is the first time the tournament was held in the Middle East.
date.11/21: is the day that A magnitude 5.6 earthquake on the Indonesian island of Java kills between 335 and 602 people.
date.11/22: is the day that A shooting at a Walmart in Chesapeake, Virginia leaves seven workers dead, including the shooter, and four others injured.
date.11/23: is the day that The last Sumatran rhinoceros in Malaysia, Imam, dies, making the species officially extinct in the country.
date.11/25: is the day that Jeddah floods: Freak rains swamp the city of Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, during an ongoing Hajj pilgrimage. Three thousand cars are swept away and 122 people perish in the torrents, with 350 others missing.
date.11/26: is the day that The Wang Fuk Court fire, a catastrophic fire in Tai Po, Hong Kong, leaves at least 168 dead and 79 injured.
date.11/27: is the day that Syrian rebel groups led by Hay'at Tahrir al-Sham launch a ground offensive into Syria.
date.11/28: is the day that Over seven hundred civilians are massacred by the Ethiopian National Defense Force and Eritrean Army in Aksum, Ethiopia.
date.11/29: is the day that The Government of Jordan officially announces the launch of the Amra City development project during a site visit by the Prime Minister.
date.11/30: is the day that The AI chatbot ChatGPT is launched by OpenAI.
date.12/1: is the day that The Arecibo Telescope collapses.
date.12/2: is the day that Cannabis is removed from the list of most dangerous drugs of the international drug control treaty by the UN Commission on Narcotic Drugs.
date.12/3: is the day that Martial law is declared in South Korea.
date.12/4: is the day that UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson is shot and killed in Manhattan, New York City near the entrance of the New York Hilton Midtown.
date.12/5: is the day that The International Olympic Committee bans Russia from competing at the 2018 Winter Olympics for doping at the 2014 Winter Olympics.
date.12/6: is the day that Donald Trump's administration officially announces the recognition of Jerusalem as the capital of Israel.
date.12/7: is the day that British racing driver Lando Norris wins the Formula One World Drivers' Championship in 2025 with McLaren.
date.12/8: is the day that Damascus falls to rebels after Syrian troops withdraw and president Bashar al-Assad leaves the country as his government collapses. Israel as a result invaded into the buffer zone between Syria and the Israeli-occupied Golan Heights.
date.12/12: is the day that Indian Grandmaster Gukesh Dommaraju became the undisputed World Chess Champion in a tournament held in Singapore, making him the 18th and the youngest champion in chess history.
date.12/13: is the day that The Treaty of Lisbon is signed by the EU member states to amend both the Treaty of Rome and the Maastricht Treaty which together form the constitutional basis of the EU. The Treaty of Lisbon is effective from 1 December 2009.
date.12/14: is the day that At least 16 people are killed, including one gunman, and 43 injured in a mass shooting during a Hanukkah celebration at Bondi Beach in the deadliest terror incident in Australia.
date.12/15: is the day that A 6.5Mw earthquake strikes the Indonesian island of Java in the city of Tasikmalaya, resulting in four deaths.
date.12/18: is the day that Argentina win the FIFA World Cup final, defeating title holders France 4–2 on penalties following a 3–3 draw after extra time.
date.12/19: is the day that Russian ambassador to Turkey Andrei Karlov is assassinated while at an art exhibition in Ankara. The assassin, Mevlüt Mert Altıntaş, is shot and killed by a Turkish guard.
date.12/20: is the day that Six people are killed and over 200 are injured when an anti-Islam activist drives a car into a crowd at a Christmas market in Magdeburg, Germany.
date.12/21: is the day that Fourteen people are killed and 25 others injured during a mass shooting at Charles University in Prague, Czech Republic.
date.12/22: is the day that A tsunami caused by an eruption of Anak Krakatau in Indonesia kills at least 430 people and injures almost a thousand more.
date.12/23: is the day that The crash of Harmony Jets Flight 185 killed all eight people on board, including the Libyan Army chief Mohammed al-Haddad in Ankara, Turkey.
date.12/24: is the day that Burmese military forces commit the Mo So massacre, killing at least 44 civilians.
date.12/25: is the day that Azerbaijan Airlines Flight 8243 crashes in Aktau, Kazakhstan, killing 38 of its occupants.
date.12/26: is the day that Israel recognises Somaliland as an independent state, becoming the first country to do so.
date.12/27: is the day that An estimated €30 million were stolen from a Sparkasse bank in Gelsenkirchen, Germany.
date.12/28: is the day that Unrest in the form of mass protests erupt across Iran, the largest uprising in the country since the Iranian Revolution.
date.12/29: is the day that Jeju Air Flight 2216 crashes into a wall in Muan, South Korea, killing 179 of the 181 occupants. It is the worst aircraft accident on South Korean soil in history.
date.12/30: is the day that A large explosion at the airport in the southern Yemeni city of Aden kills at least 22 people and wounds 50.
date.12/31: is the day that The World Health Organization issues its first emergency use validation for a COVID-19 vaccine.

year.2026: is the year that Bulgaria officaly adopts the Euro, becoming the 21st Eurozone country.
year.2025: is the year that Fourteen people are killed and 57 others injured during a vehicle-ramming and shooting attack in New Orleans, Louisiana.
year.2024: is the year that A 7.5 Mww  earthquake strikes the western coast of Japan, killing more than 500 people and injuring over 1,000 others. A majority of direct deaths were due to collapsed homes.
year.2023: is the year that Croatia officially adopts the Euro, becoming the 20th Eurozone country, and becomes the 27th member of the Schengen Area.
year.2017: is the year that An attack on a nightclub in Istanbul, Turkey, during New Year's celebrations, kills 39 people and injures 79 others.
year.2015: is the year that The Eurasian Economic Union comes into effect, creating a political and economic union between Russia, Belarus, Armenia, Kazakhstan and Kyrgyzstan.
year.2013: is the year that At least 60 people are killed and 200 injured in a stampede after celebrations at Félix Houphouët-Boigny Stadium in Abidjan, Ivory Coast.
year.2011: is the year that A bomb explodes as Coptic Christians in Alexandria, Egypt, leave a new year service, killing 23 people.
year.2010: is the year that A suicide car bomber detonates at a volleyball tournament in Lakki Marwat, Pakistan, killing 105 and injuring 100 more.
year.2009: is the year that Sixty-six people die in a nightclub fire in Bangkok, Thailand.
year.2007: is the year that Bulgaria and Romania join the EU.
year.2004: is the year that In a vote of confidence, General Pervez Musharraf wins 658 out of 1,170 votes in the Electoral College of Pakistan, and according to Article 41(8) of the Constitution of Pakistan, is "deemed to be elected" to the office of President until October 2007.
year.2001: is the year that Greece adopts the Euro, becoming the 12th Eurozone country.
year.1999: is the year that The Euro currency is introduced in 11 member nations of the European Union (with the exception of the United Kingdom, Denmark, Greece and Sweden).
year.1998: is the year that Following a currency reform, Russia begins to circulate new rubles to stem inflation and promote confidence.
year.1995: is the year that The World Trade Organization comes into being.
year.1994: is the year that The Zapatista Army of National Liberation initiates twelve days of armed conflict in the Mexican state of Chiapas.
year.1993: is the year that Dissolution of Czechoslovakia: Czechoslovakia is divided into the Czech Republic and Slovak Republic.
year.1990: is the year that David Dinkins is sworn in as New York City's first black mayor.
year.1989: is the year that The Montreal Protocol comes into force, stopping the use of chemicals contributing to ozone depletion.
year.1988: is the year that The Evangelical Lutheran Church in America comes into existence, creating the largest Lutheran denomination in the United States.
year.1987: is the year that The Isleta Pueblo tribe elect Verna Williamson to be their first female governor.
year.1985: is the year that The first British mobile phone call is made by Michael Harrison to his father Sir Ernest Harrison, chairman of Vodafone.
year.1984: is the year that The original American Telephone & Telegraph Company is divested of its 22 Bell System companies as a result of the settlement of the 1974 United States Department of Justice antitrust suit against AT&T.
year.1983: is the year that The ARPANET officially changes to using TCP/IP, the Internet Protocol, effectively creating the Internet.
year.1982: is the year that Peruvian Javier Pérez de Cuéllar becomes the first Latin American to hold the title of Secretary-General of the United Nations.
year.1981: is the year that Greece is admitted into the European Community.
year.1979: is the year that the Joint Communiqué on the Establishment of Diplomatic Relations and Taiwan Relations Act enter into force. Through the Communiqué, the United States establishes normal diplomatic relations with China. Through the Act, the United States guarantees military support for Taiwan.
year.1978: is the year that Air India Flight 855, a Boeing 747, crashes into the Arabian Sea off the coast of Bombay, India, due to instrument failure, spatial disorientation, and pilot error, killing all 213 people on board.
year.1976: is the year that A bomb explodes on board Middle East Airlines Flight 438 over Qaisumah, Saudi Arabia, killing all 81 people on board.
year.1973: is the year that Denmark, Ireland and the United Kingdom are admitted into the European Economic Community.
year.1971: is the year that Cigarette advertisements are banned on American television.
year.1970: is the year that The defined beginning of Unix time, at 00:00:00.
year.1965: is the year that The People's Democratic Party of Afghanistan is founded in Kabul, Afghanistan.
year.1964: is the year that The Federation of Rhodesia and Nyasaland is divided into the independent republics of Zambia and Malawi, and the British-controlled Rhodesia.
year.1962: is the year that Western Samoa achieves independence from New Zealand; its name is changed to the Independent State of Western Samoa.
year.1960: is the year that Cameroon achieves independence from France and the United Kingdom.
year.1959: is the year that Cuban Revolution: Fulgencio Batista, dictator of Cuba, is overthrown by Fidel Castro's forces.
year.1958: is the year that The European Economic Community is established.
year.1957: is the year that George Town, Penang, is made a city by a royal charter of Queen Elizabeth II of the United Kingdom.
year.1956: is the year that Sudan achieves independence from Egypt and the United Kingdom.
year.1949: is the year that United Nations cease-fire takes effect in Kashmir from one minute before midnight. War between India and Pakistan stops accordingly.
year.1948: is the year that The British railway network is nationalized to form British Railways.
year.1947: is the year that Cold War: The American and British occupation zones in Allied-occupied Germany, after World War II, merge to form the Bizone, which later (with the French zone) became part of West Germany.
year.1945: is the year that World War II: The German Luftwaffe launches Operation Bodenplatte, a massive, but failed, attempt to knock out Allied air power in northern Europe in a single blow.
year.1942: is the year that The Declaration by United Nations is signed by twenty-six nations.
year.1934: is the year that Alcatraz Island in San Francisco Bay becomes a United States federal prison.
year.1932: is the year that The United States Post Office Department issues a set of 12 stamps commemorating the 200th anniversary of George Washington's birth.
year.1929: is the year that The former municipalities of Point Grey, British Columbia and South Vancouver, British Columbia are amalgamated into Vancouver.
year.1928: is the year that Boris Bazhanov defects through Iran to seek asylum in France. He is the only member of Joseph Stalin's secretariat to have defected from the Soviet Union.
year.1927: is the year that New Mexican oil legislation goes into effect, leading to the formal outbreak of the Cristero War.
year.1923: is the year that Britain's Railways are grouped into the Big Four: LNER, GWR, SR, and LMS.
year.1914: is the year that The SPT Airboat Line becomes the world's first scheduled airline to use a winged aircraft.
year.1912: is the year that The Republic of China is established.
year.1910: is the year that Captain David Beatty is promoted to rear admiral, and becomes the youngest admiral in the Royal Navy (except for royal family members) since Horatio Nelson.
year.1902: is the year that The first American college football bowl game, the Rose Bowl between Michigan and Stanford, is held in Pasadena, California.
year.1901: is the year that The Southern Nigeria Protectorate is established within the British Empire.
year.1900: is the year that Nigeria becomes a British protectorate with Frederick Lugard as high commissioner.
year.1899: is the year that Spanish rule ends in Cuba.
year.1898: is the year that New York, New York annexes land from surrounding counties, creating the City of Greater New York. The four initial boroughs, Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens, and The Bronx, are joined on January 25 by Staten Island to create the modern city of five boroughs.
year.1892: is the year that Ellis Island begins processing immigrants into the United States.
year.1890: is the year that Eritrea is consolidated into a colony by the Italian government as Italian Eritrea.
year.1885: is the year that Twenty-five nations adopt Sandford Fleming's proposal for standard time (and also, time zones).
year.1877: is the year that Queen Victoria of the United Kingdom is proclaimed Empress of India.
year.1863: is the year that American Civil War: The Emancipation Proclamation takes effect in Confederate territory.
year.1861: is the year that Liberal forces supporting Benito Juárez enter Mexico City.
year.1860: is the year that The first Polish postage stamp is issued, replacing the Russian stamps previously in use.
year.1847: is the year that The world's first "Mercy" Hospital is founded in Pittsburgh, United States, by a group of Sisters of Mercy from Ireland; the name will go on to grace over 30 major hospitals throughout the world.
year.1845: is the year that The Philippines moves its national calendar to align with other Asian countries' calendars by skipping Tuesday, December 31, 1844. The change has been ordered by Governor–General Narciso Claveria to reform the country's calendar so that it aligns with the rest of Asia. Its territory has been one day behind the rest of Asia for 323 years since the arrival of Ferdinand Magellan in the Philippines on March 16, 1521.
year.1834: is the year that Most of Germany forms the Zollverein customs union, the first such union between sovereign states.
year.1822: is the year that The Greek Constitution of 1822 is adopted by the First National Assembly at Epidaurus.
year.1818: is the year that Mary Wollstonecraft Shelley (anonymously) publishes the pioneering work of science fiction, Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus, in London.
year.1810: is the year that Major-General Lachlan Macquarie officially becomes Governor of New South Wales.
year.1808: is the year that The United States bans the importation of slaves.
year.1806: is the year that The French Republican Calendar is abolished.
year.1804: is the year that French rule ends in Haiti. Haiti becomes the first black-majority republic and second independent country in North America after the United States.
year.1801: is the year that The legislative union of Kingdom of Great Britain and Kingdom of Ireland is completed, and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland is proclaimed.
year.1788: is the year that The first edition of The Times of London, previously The Daily Universal Register, is published.
year.1781: is the year that American Revolutionary War: One thousand five hundred soldiers of the 6th Pennsylvania Regiment under General Anthony Wayne's command rebel against the Continental Army's winter camp in Morristown, New Jersey in the Pennsylvania Line Mutiny of 1781.
year.1776: is the year that American Revolutionary War: Burning of Norfolk – Norfolk, Virginia, is burned to the ground by combined Royal Navy and Continental Army action.
year.1773: is the year that The hymn that becomes known as "Amazing Grace", previously titled "1 Chronicles 17:16–17, Faith's Review and Expectation", is first used to accompany a sermon led by John Newton in the town of Olney, Buckinghamshire, England.
year.1772: is the year that The first traveler's cheques, which could be used in 90 European cities, are issued by the London Credit Exchange Company.
year.1739: is the year that Bouvet Island, the world's remotest island, is discovered by French explorer Jean-Baptiste Charles Bouvet de Lozier.
year.1726: is the year that J. S. Bach leads the first performance of Herr Gott, dich loben wir, BWV 16, his church cantata for New Year's Day to a libretto by Georg Christian Lehms.
year.1725: is the year that J. S. Bach leads the first performance of his chorale cantata Jesu, nun sei gepreiset, BWV 41, which features the trumpet fanfares from the beginning also in the end.
year.1707: is the year that John V is proclaimed King of Portugal and the Algarves in Lisbon.
year.1700: is the year that Russia begins using the Anno Domini era instead of the Anno Mundi era of the Byzantine Empire.
year.1651: is the year that Charles II is crowned King of Scotland at Scone Palace.
year.1604: is the year that The Masque of Indian and China Knights is performed by courtiers of James VI and I at Hampton Court.
year.1600: is the year that Scotland recognises January 1 as the start of the year, instead of March 25.
year.1527: is the year that Croatian nobles elect Ferdinand I, Archduke of Austria as King of Croatia in the 1527 election in Cetin.
year.1515: is the year that Twenty-year-old Francis, Duke of Brittany, succeeds to the French throne following the death of his father-in-law, Louis XII.
year.1502: is the year that The present-day location of Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, is first explored by the Portuguese.
year.1438: is the year that Albert II of Habsburg is crowned King of Hungary.
year.1259: is the year that Michael VIII Palaiologos is proclaimed co-emperor of the Empire of Nicaea with his ward John IV Laskaris.
year.1068: is the year that Romanos IV Diogenes marries Eudokia Makrembolitissa and is crowned Byzantine Emperor.
year.1001: is the year that Grand Prince Stephen I of Hungary is named the first King of Hungary by Pope Sylvester II (probable).
year.947: is the year that Emperor Taizong of the Khitan-led Liao dynasty captures Daliang, ending the dynasty and empire of the Later Jin.
year.417: is the year that Emperor Honorius forces Galla Placidia into marriage to Constantius, his famous general (magister militum) (probable).
year.404: is the year that Saint Telemachus tries to stop a gladiatorial fight in a Roman amphitheatre, and is stoned to death by the crowd. This act impresses the Christian Emperor Honorius, who issues a historic ban on gladiatorial fights.
year.193: is the year that The Senate chooses Pertinax against his will to succeed Commodus as Roman emperor.
year.-42: is the year that The Roman Senate posthumously deifies Julius Caesar.
year.-45: is the year that The Julian calendar takes effect as the civil calendar of the Roman Republic, establishing January 1 as the new date of the new year.
year.-153: is the year that For the first time, Roman consuls begin their year in office on January 1.
year.2022: is the year that Massive nationwide protests and unrest break out in Kazakhstan over the sudden increase of liquefied petroleum gas prices, leading to 238 people dead and thousands injured by January 11.
year.1991: is the year that Sharon Pratt Dixon becomes the first African American woman mayor of a major city and first woman Mayor of the District of Columbia.
year.1975: is the year that At the opening of a new railway line, a bomb blast at Samastipur, Bihar, India, fatally wounds Lalit Narayan Mishra, Minister of Railways.
year.1967: is the year that Ronald Reagan, past movie actor and future President of the United States, is sworn in as Governor of California.
year.1963: is the year that Vietnam War: The Viet Cong wins its first major victory, at the Battle of Ap Bac.
year.1955: is the year that Following the assassination of the Panamanian president José Antonio Remón Cantera, his deputy, José Ramón Guizado, takes power, but is quickly deposed after his involvement in Cantera's death is discovered.
year.1954: is the year that India establishes its highest civilian awards, the Bharat Ratna and the Padma Vibhushan.
year.1941: is the year that World War II: The Cardiff Blitz severely damages the cathedral in Cardiff, Wales.
year.1921: is the year that World premiere of the science fiction play R.U.R. by the Czech writer Karel Čapek in a theater in Hradec Králové.
year.1920: is the year that The second Palmer Raid, ordered by the US Department of Justice, results in 6,000 suspected communists and anarchists being arrested and held without trial.
year.1865: is the year that Uruguayan War: The Siege of Paysandú ends as the Brazilians and Coloradans capture Paysandú, Uruguay.
year.1791: is the year that Northwest Indian War: The Big Bottom massacre is committed by Lenape and Wyandot warriors in the Ohio Country, North America.
year.1777: is the year that American Revolutionary War: American forces under the command of General George Washington repulse a British attack led by General Charles Cornwallis at the Battle of the Assunpink Creek near Trenton, New Jersey.
year.1680: is the year that Trunajaya rebellion: Amangkurat II of Mataram and his bodyguards execute the rebel leader Trunajaya.
year.1492: is the year that Reconquista: The Emirate of Granada, the last Moorish stronghold in Spain, surrenders.
year.1444: is the year that Christian forces defeat the Turks in the battle of Kunovica.
year.533: is the year that Mercurius becomes Pope John II, the first pope to adopt a new name upon elevation to the papacy.
year.366: is the year that The Alemanni cross the frozen Rhine in large numbers, invading the Roman Empire.
year.69: is the year that The Roman legions in Germania Superior refuse to swear loyalty to Galba. They rebel and proclaim Vitellius as emperor.
year.2020: is the year that Iranian General Qasem Soleimani is killed by an American airstrike near Baghdad International Airport, igniting global concerns of a potential armed conflict.
year.2019: is the year that Chang'e 4 makes the first soft landing on the far side of the Moon, deploying the Yutu-2 lunar rover.
year.2018: is the year that For the first time in history, all five major storm surge gates in the Netherlands are closed simultaneously in the wake of a storm.
year.2016: is the year that In response to the execution of Nimr al-Nimr, Iran ends its diplomatic relations with Saudi Arabia.
year.2002: is the year that Israeli–Palestinian conflict: Israeli forces seize the Palestinian freighter Karine A in the Red Sea, finding 50 tons of weapons.
year.1992: is the year that CommutAir Flight 4821 crashes on approach to Adirondack Regional Airport, in Saranac Lake, New York, killing two people.
year.1977: is the year that Apple Computer is incorporated.
year.1961: is the year that Cold War: After a series of economic retaliations against one another, the United States severs diplomatic relations with Cuba.
year.1953: is the year that Frances P. Bolton and her son, Oliver from Ohio, become the first mother and son to serve simultaneously in the U.S. Congress.
year.1946: is the year that Popular Canadian American jockey George Woolf suffers a concussion during a freak racing accident; he dies from the injury the following day. The annual George Woolf Memorial Jockey Award is created to honor him.
year.1944: is the year that World War II: US flying ace Major Greg "Pappy" Boyington is shot down in his Vought F4U Corsair by Captain Masajiro Kawato flying a Mitsubishi A6M Zero.
year.1933: is the year that Minnie D. Craig becomes the first woman elected as Speaker of the North Dakota House of Representatives, the first woman to hold a Speaker position anywhere in the United States.
year.1913: is the year that An Atlantic coast storm sets the lowest confirmed barometric pressure reading (955.0 mb (28.20 inHg)) for a non-tropical system in the continental United States.
year.1911: is the year that A magnitude 7.7 earthquake destroys the city of Almaty in Russian Turkestan.
year.1871: is the year that In the Battle of Bapaume, an engagement in the Franco-Prussian War, General Louis Faidherbe's forces bring about a Prussian retreat.
year.1870: is the year that Construction work begins on the Brooklyn Bridge in New York, United States.
year.1868: is the year that Meiji Restoration in Japan: The Tokugawa shogunate is abolished; agents of Satsuma and Chōshū seize power.
year.1848: is the year that Joseph Jenkins Roberts is sworn in as the first president of Liberia.
year.1833: is the year that Captain James Onslow, in the Clio, reasserts British sovereignty over the Falkland Islands.
year.1815: is the year that Austria, the United Kingdom, and France form a secret defensive alliance against Prussia and Russia.
year.1749: is the year that Benning Wentworth issues the first of the New Hampshire Grants, leading to the establishment of Vermont.
year.1653: is the year that By the Coonan Cross Oath, the Eastern Church in India cuts itself off from colonial Portuguese tutelage.
year.1521: is the year that Pope Leo X excommunicates Martin Luther in the papal bull Decet Romanum Pontificem.
year.250: is the year that Emperor Decius orders everyone in the Roman Empire (except Jews) to make sacrifices to the Roman gods.
year.2008: is the year that A Let L-410 Turbolet crashes in the Los Roques Archipelago in Venezuela, killing 14 people.
year.2006: is the year that Ehud Olmert becomes acting Prime Minister of Israel after the incumbent, Ariel Sharon, suffers a second, apparently more serious stroke.
year.2000: is the year that A Norwegian passenger train departing from Trondheim, collides with a local train coming from Hamar in Åsta in Åmot Municipality; 19 people are killed and 68 injured in the accident.
year.1972: is the year that Rose Heilbron becomes the first female judge to sit at the Old Bailey in London, UK.
year.1951: is the year that Korean War: Chinese and North Korean forces capture Seoul for the second time.
year.1918: is the year that The Finnish Declaration of Independence is recognized by Russia, Sweden, Germany and France.
year.1909: is the year that Explorer Aeneas Mackintosh of the Imperial Trans-Antarctic Expedition escaped death by fleeing across ice floes.
year.1903: is the year that Topsy, an elephant, is electrocuted by the owners of Luna Park, Coney Island. The Edison film company records the film Electrocuting an Elephant of Topsy's death.
year.1896: is the year that Utah is admitted as the 45th U.S. state.
year.1884: is the year that The Fabian Society is founded in London, United Kingdom.
year.1878: is the year that Russo-Turkish War (1877–78): Sofia is liberated from Ottoman rule.
year.1854: is the year that The McDonald Islands are discovered by Captain William McDonald aboard the Samarang.
year.1853: is the year that After having been kidnapped and sold into slavery in the American South, Solomon Northup regains his freedom; his memoir Twelve Years a Slave later becomes a national bestseller.
year.1844: is the year that The first issue of the Swedish-languaged Saima newspaper founded by J. V. Snellman is published in Kuopio, Finland.
year.1798: is the year that Constantine Hangerli arrives in Bucharest, Wallachia, as its new Prince, invested by the Ottoman Empire.
year.1762: is the year that Great Britain declares war on Spain, which meant the entry of Spain into the Seven Years' War.
year.1717: is the year that The Netherlands, Great Britain, and France sign the Triple Alliance.
year.1649: is the year that English Civil War: The Rump Parliament votes to put Charles I on trial.
year.1642: is the year that English Civil War: King Charles I, accompanied by 400 soldiers, attempts to arrest five members of Parliament for treason, only to discover the men had been tipped off and fled.
year.871: is the year that Battle of Reading: Æthelred of Wessex and his brother Alfred are defeated by a Danish invasion army.
year.-46: is the year that Julius Caesar fights Titus Labienus in the Battle of Ruspina.
year.2014: is the year that A launch of the communication satellite GSAT-14 aboard the GSLV MK.II D5 marks the first successful flight of an Indian cryogenic engine.
year.2005: is the year that The dwarf planet Eris is discovered by Palomar Observatory-based astronomers, later motivating the International Astronomical Union (IAU) to define the term planet for the first time.
year.2003: is the year that A suicide bombing at the Tel Aviv central bus station kills 23 people and injures over 100 more.
year.1969: is the year that The Venera 5 space probe is launched at 06:28:08 UTC from Baikonur.
